title: Optimisation of Cosolvent Concentration for Topical Drug Delivery – II: Influence of Propylene Glycol on Ibuprofen Permeation

anchor_evidence.snippet: Table 6. Steady-state fluxes, thermodynamic and kinetic parameters and permeability coefficients of ibuprofen through human epidermis from saturated formulations containing ethanol, PG and water (mean 8 SD, n = 5)

Table 5. Steady-state fluxes, thermodynamic and kinetic parameters and permeability coefficients of ibuprofen through human epidermis from saturated formulations containing PG and water (mean 8 SD, n = 5)

notes: This study investigates the impact of propylene glycol on ibuprofen permeation through skin, using various solvent systems and including detailed methodological descriptions.

title: Optimisation of Cosolvent Concentration for Topical Drug Delivery III – Influence of Lipophilic Vehicles on Ibuprofen Permeation

anchor_evidence.snippet: Table 4. Steady-state flux values and permeability coefficients for the permeation of ibuprofen from saturated formulations containing MO and MG 812 through human epidermis.
Table 3. Steady-state flux values and permeability coefficients for the permeation of ibuprofen from saturated formulations containing MO and/or MG through silicone membrane.

notes: Study extends prior work on hydrophilic systems to lipophilic vehicles with a focus on ibuprofen.
